title: Meituan's LongCat-2.0 is the first 1.6T model trained end-to-end on Chinese chips
subtitle: The open-weight coding model matches near-frontier benchmarks and was pre-trained across 35 trillion tokens on a 50,000-card domestic ASIC cluster — with no rollbacks, the paper claims.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Chinese food-delivery-and-everything-else giant Meituan open-sourced LongCat-2.0, a 1.6 trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model, with weights available under the meituan-longcat organization on Hugging Face. The model activates roughly 33 to 56 billion parameters per token and carries a 1 million-token context window. On benchmarks reported at release it scored 59.5 on SWE-bench Pro and 70.8 on Terminal-Bench, above GPT-5.5 on the coding benchmark and approaching leading closed models on the terminal one. Meituan's paper reports pretraining ran across more than 35 trillion tokens with no rollbacks or irrecoverable loss spikes — a claim analysts flagged this week as one of the release's most consequential lines.

The claim that matters is not the benchmark. It is the hardware. Meituan says LongCat-2.0 was pre-trained and served end-to-end on a 50,000-card cluster of domestic Chinese ASICs, without NVIDIA GPUs or Google TPUs anywhere in the loop. If independently verified, this is the first time a model at this scale has completed both training and inference outside the US semiconductor supply chain. Chinese-media coverage this week, echoed by SCMP and VentureBeat, has emphasized that this closes the loop the US export-control regime was designed to keep open: cut off from H100s and their successors, Chinese labs have shipped a working substitute.

The story fits a pattern that has been visible for six months. DeepSeek-V4-Pro dropped in April with training-cost claims that reordered the industry conversation. Moonshot raised at a twenty billion dollar valuation on the back of Kimi K2.6's benchmark showing. Alibaba's Qwen3-Max-Thinking has been trading blows with US frontier models. Now Meituan is playing the hardware sovereignty card explicitly. The through-line is that Chinese labs are pursuing three-dimensional parity — capability, price, and supply chain independence — and are willing to open-weight the results to make the argument in public. The remaining question is whether US labs' closed-model efficiency lead is worth more than open-weight distribution at parity.

A takeaway for learners: run the open weights yourself before you accept the benchmarks. LongCat-2.0 is on Hugging Face; the tools to load and test it exist on a rented GPU or a modest local cluster. Anyone can now build against a 1.6 trillion-parameter model that did not require an export license. If you are learning agentic coding — the actual workflow of letting a model plan and edit repositories — pulling LongCat and comparing it head-to-head against Claude Code or Codex on a real task is the fastest way to understand where the frontier actually is, not where the press releases say it is.

sources.title: Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T near-frontier agentic coding model — VentureBeat
